Off Calendar Jul-14-2015 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
Off Calendar Jul-14-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun & Dave Parnall for and with James Bennett. Michael Beuselinck for Patricia Gridley. Adrian Sawyer & Jeff Baumann for and with Michael Gridley and for Robert Gridley.

Re: Motion to Strike Costs:
Ms. Calhoun stated that she is arguing on the issue of interest and that Michael & Robert have requested interest for a time period that interest was ordered frozen. Mr. Sawyer stated that the order is not clear as to what Judge Wiss intended, that interest is mandatory by statute, cited 685.010 and says that there is no basis to reduce the requested amount of interest. Ms. Calhoun stated that there was no remedy taken on the disputed order, that this order is what the parties have been relying on as well as two minute orders issued in the ENGIT matter. Mr. Sawyer stated that the order does not say they cannot request interest, that he does not believe Judge Wiss would do something she had no power to do and that if it is outside the jurisdiction of the court no remedy would be necessary.

Re: Motion for Attorney's fees:
Ms. Calhoun stated that they had issue with granting $34,151.75 in attorney's fees and with the other categories of fees, that they are attempting to run fees already disallowed, that they need clarity on the matter prior to mediation, that fees should not be used to collect judgment, that Michael had control of more than enough money to satisfy the judgment and that he is trying to increase the judgment instead of paying it off. Mr. Sawyer stated that Michael has incurred thousands of dollars in attorney's fees, that $230,000.00 has already been ruled on and that he has incurred $40,000.00 in additional attorney's fees in the last couple of years. Ms. Calhoun argued that the attorney's fees were paid from ENGIT funds, that Christine & Patricia at 50% beneficiaries of that fund and that Michael used ENGIT as his personal bank account. Mr. Sawyer stated that the source of payment is immaterial, that they filed a timely creditor claim and that the claim is reduced to a judgment. Ms. Calhoun stated that Mr. Bennett has limited powers and cannot accept or reject creditor claims and cited 686.010.
The court requested that the parties submit proposed order by July 8, 2015 and that the Motion to strike costs and the Motion for attorney's fees will be taken under submission as of that date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Saephan. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Feb-11-2015 Continued To Feb-20-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following cases were heard: PES-92-259147 Estate of Elsie N. Gridley and PES-04-286102 Estate of Arnold Gridley.
Appearances: James Wagstaffe for and with (trustee) Michael Gridley. G. Scott Haislet for and with (beneficiary) Patricia Gridley. Emilie Calhoun & Dave Parnall for and with (special administrator of Christine Bennett's estate) James Bennett.

Mr. Wagstaffe provided case history/background, referred to prior Judicial Officers decisions/opinions, prior settlement agreements and prior court orders. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with respect to trustees fees, attorney fees, extraordinary fees for both trustee and attorney, the 9th accounting not balancing and whether or not it is in proper format, lack of documentation to support time entries, whether or not the court has jurisdiction over the trust, whether the accounting should include all reimbursements, whether the judgment belongs to the trust/should be a trust asset, etc. The parties all agree that a further accounting will be needed. Mediation was discussed. Ms. Calhoun provided Mr. Wagstaffe with an oral list of items she is requesting. Mr. Wagstaffe agreed to provide counsel with information regarding valuation of the Vacaville property. The petition for trustees extraordinary fees and the motion for offset were continued to February 20, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for counsel to submit additional briefs by February 18, 2015. Said matters will be under submission on that date. The accounting petition and the motion for attorney's fees were continued to March 20, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Wagstaffe to provide additional information that may be helpful.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Clark. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On The Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt
Off Calendar Jul-31-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ivo Labar for Michael Gridley and Robert Gridley.

The Court noted that no appearance was made for Patricia Gridley. Mr. Labar stated that there was no word or communication from anyone, that there were no discussions and that the stipulations reached in the other civil matter do not apply to this matter. The Court provided the case history, referred to the December 21, 2011 Order Granting Motion to Compel responses to the first set of special interrogatories and request for production of documents, stated that no return was received, that nothing was filed by Patricia Gridley and that there was nothing to show that the order was complied with. The Court also noted that Mr. Haislet filed a Notice of Unavailability (June 22, 2012 through July 13, 2012) on June 20, 2012 subsequent to the Order to Show Cause having been issued and that no application for continuance of this matter had been filed. The Court finds Patricia Gridley in contempt. Neither the Motion to Compel Discovery nor the Application for An Order to Show Cause was opposed by Patricia Gridley. Nor did she appear. Patricia Gridley is ordered to pay a $1,000.00 fine and Attorney's fees of $1,536.00 to Michael Gridley and Robert Gridley. Mr. Labar is to modify the order to recite the history.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Ruiz.

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for OSC re: Contempt as to the OEX on Patricia Gridley.
Dept. 514, Judge Donald Sullivan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Lana L. Loper.
Kelly Corcoran, Esq. for Judgment Creditors: Michael and Robert Gridley.
G. Scott Haislet, Esq. for and with Judgment Debtor: Patricia Gridley.
The Court hears testimony and arguments from counsel and Mrs. Gridley and after hearing the arguments and considering of the moving papers and responses finds that Debtor refusal to answer to the relevant questions at the Examinations on 12-01-11, 12-20-11 and 01-25-12 were not justified and did not comply with CCP 1211 and CCP 1219. The Court finds direct contempt, ordered Mrs. Gridley to be remanded to the custody of the S.F. Sheriff without bail; granted sanction in the amount of $1,000.00, attorney's fees and costs in the amount of $7,501.90. Further, continued the matter and stayed the sentencing until 05-04-12 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 514 for Mrs. Gridley to re-consider.
Ms. Corcoran to prepare an Order and submit to Dept. 514 for signature.

Mr. Livingston stated that the accountants agreed to reduce their fees by $5,000.00. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the need for an evidentiary hearing on the pending creditor's claims, whether distribution in the amount of $40,000.00 should be paid to Ms. Bennett or held until the outcome of the appeal, spoke of the issue of sucharges against Ms. Bennett, the outstanding Attorney's awards, reported that there was a stipulation regarding the tax matter and stated that they were working delingently to resolve the matter. Counsel will hold further discussions regarding Ms. Bennet's distribution. The petition regarding the creditor's claims was continued to October 15, 2010 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an evidentiary hearing. The accounting petitions were also continued to that date for trial setting. Mr. Livingston's ex parte application was denied without prejudice. Mr. Koplowitz will prepare and circulate an order with regard to the above excluding the ruling on the ex parte application.

Ex Parte Application For Court Approval To Use Estate Funds For Cablecar Advertisers Inc.
Off Calendar Feb-05-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Livingston for and with Phillip Wright and for Christine Bennett. Jim Wagstaff and Nelson Wild for Robert Gridley and Michael Gridley. Richard Wise for Dorothy Diez.

Mr. Livingston stated that they received a new letter of intent regarding the sale of the business one week ago, and that the company needed more support. In addition, Mr.Livingston stated that the amount he was requesting was for rent and insurance for January, February and March, that rent was $20,000.00 per month, that insurance was from $16,500.00 to $19,000.00 per month, and that the business must be operating in order to sell. Mr. Wagstaff expressed that loss of money was the primary concern, that this was not the first potential sale, and suggested putting the matter out for one month. Mr. Wise represented on behalf of Ms. Diez that this was not the time to shut down the business or shut off the money. Mr. Livingston reported that the earliest closing date on the sale was March 15th. Upon the suggestion of the Court, Mr. Livingston agreed to speak to all Counsel to provide more detail regarding the sale which is to kept confidential amongst counsel only. The Court allowed $80,000.00 from the estate to be used for the business. Mr. Livingston will file a progress report in 30 days and prepare the order.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR#8098. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-05-2007 Continued To Dec-10-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Wise for Dorothy Diez. Dennis Livinston for Christine Bennett and Phillip Wright (Co-Executors). William Dillingham for Co-Administrators. Keith Fong (Co-Counsel) with Nelson Wild for Michael and Robert Gridley (Petitioners). Phillip Wright is present.

Mr. Wild responded to the Ex-Parte petition stating that he was served, but it was inadequate, that he had no chance to look at it, that the November 8th offer was not known , that the company lost money, and that they didn't know what the business was worth. Mr. Wise stated that Ms. Diez was concerned about money being poured in, and that she did not oppose the Ex-Parte. Mr. Livingston reported that an offer had came in , but it was still in discussion, that he couldn't release any information due to the confidentiality issue, but he was willing to produce information in camera, and that the exclusivity agreement period would be over at the end of the month. Mr. Wise suggested the Court appoint an independent expert. The Court approved the use of $32,325.35 for November, but gives no authority to spend any more money after that (signing the order for the same). The Court did not make a determination on the first right of refusal issue at this time. The Court will treat the Motion to Compel as a 9613 petition for the Court to instruct the Personal Representative to take certain action. Granted as to Mr. Livingston providing the balance sheets for 2007 to Mr. Fong. Mr. Fong will prepare the Order. The issue is moot as to disclosure to the purchaser. The accounting issues are still outstanding and continued to December 10, 2007. The Court would like Mr. Livingston to have an updated status by then. Mr. Wild and Mr. Wise should file a summary as to what the outstanding accounting issues are by December 3, 2007.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Felicia Smith

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of 1st Account And Report
Off Calendar Mar-19-2007 Continued To Mar-26-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nelson Wild (Counsel for Robert Gridley and Michael Gridley, Objectors); Richard Wise (Counsel for Doris Diez, Objector); Dennis Livingston (Counsel for Christine Bennett and Phillip Wright, Co-Personal Representatives).

Mr. Livingston advised the Court that he needed a small amount of time to file the accounting but would like to keep the schedule made at the last hearing. Mr. Wild expressed the reservation that depending upon when the accounting was actually filed, he might need additional time within which to file a response or objections. Having considered the same, the Court continued the status hearing on the accounting to March 26, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 and retained the original schedule set at the last hearing. However, the accounting is to be served on opposing counsel by personal service and Mr. Wild's reply date will be extended one day for each day the accounting is filed past the original schedule filing date.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Rigo Guitron; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of 1st Account And Report
Off Calendar Mar-12-2007 Continued To Mar-19-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Livingston (Counsel for Christine Bennett, Personal Representative); Nelson Wild (Counsel for Robert Gridley and Michael Gridley, Objectors); Michael Gridley (Objector - present in court).

Discussion concerning the status of the case, primarily the accounting, was had. The Court determined that one set of copies of the financial records for the decedent's cable car business would be provided to Mr. Wild and his clients at the expense of the estate.

Mr. Livingston represented that the accounting would be filed with the Court within the next three days. Based upon that representation, the Court revised the previous filing and hearing schedule as follows (all appearances at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 unless otherwise noted):

Status hearing for filing accounting: March 19, 2007 (no appearances required if accounting is filed).
Hearing on the Accounting: April 9, 2007 (Mr. Livingston is to receive the Courtroom Clerk's authorization for that date prior to filing the Petition with the filing clerk in Room 103).

Date by which objections to the accounting are to be filed and served: April 2, 2007.

The Trial Status Conference previously scheduled for March 26, 2007 is vacated.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Rigo Guitron; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of 1st Account And Report
Off Calendar Feb-26-2007 Continued To Mar-12-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Livingston (Counsel for Christine Bennett, Personal Representative); George Benetatos (Counsel for Patricia Gridley); Richard Wise (Counsel for Doris Diez); Nelson Wild (Counsel for Michael Gridley and Robert Gridley, Petitioners).

After discussion concerning the status of the accounting and status report, Mr. Livingston is to have the accounting and status report on file by March 12, 2007. He is to request, and the Courtroom Clerk is instructed to authorize, a hearing date of April 2, 2007 on the accounting. Objections, if any, to the accounting must be filed on or before March 26, 2007. The current status hearing on the accounting is continued to March 12, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. No appearance is necessary if the accounting is filed prior to that date.

There then ensued a protracted discussion concerning the viability of the current trial date. While the Court did not specifically rule on Mr. Wild's contention that Mr. Benetatos' client lacked standing or interest to join in the objections of Christine Bennett to the Petition, the Court ordered the matter to the Presiding Judge for trial setting (signing the Order for same in open court) and took the current trial date off calendar.

Judicial Officer: Dorothy McMath, Judge Pro Tempore; Reporter: Katie Bersamin CSR# 11722; Bailiff: Jimmy Peralta; Court Examiner: None; Court Clerk: R. Michael Diles.
